mnemonics: HARMS: Hospital, Agriculture, Research, Mortuary, Sanatorium
keyPoints: Major sources include hospitals, clinics, and diagnostic laboratories.
Smaller sources like dental clinics, veterinary hospitals, and blood banks also contribute significantly.
Households generate biomedical waste through expired medicines and used medical supplies like syringes.
commonMistakes: Assuming only large hospitals generate biomedical waste, ignoring smaller sources like home healthcare.
Incorrectly classifying regular municipal solid waste (like food scraps) as biomedical waste.
Failing to segregate different types of biomedical waste at the source, leading to improper treatment.
